recommendations.reasoning: The definitive memory-and-grief sci-fi romance—Gondry's surreal, heartbreaking meditation on erasing painful memories only to realize they're worth keeping.
Villeneuve's contemplative masterpiece about a linguist processing grief through alien contact; slow-burn pacing with profound emotional weight about time and loss.
Duncan Jones' lonely, introspective chamber piece about an astronaut confronting identity and memory at the end of a three-year moon mission.
Soderbergh's haunting adaptation of Solaris—a grieving psychologist encounters his dead wife aboard a space station orbiting a mysterious planet.
Atmospheric slow-burn about a scientist building AI to resurrect his deceased wife; moody visuals and a meditation on grief, memory, and letting go.
Alex Garland's hypnotic, unsettling journey into a reality-warping zone; a biologist searches for her missing husband while confronting loss and self-destruction.
Von Trier's apocalyptic depression study—two sisters grapple with existential dread and fractured relationships as a rogue planet approaches Earth.
Shane Carruth's enigmatic puzzle-box about identity, memory, and connection; demands patience but rewards with a deeply moody, meditative experience.
